block_rows.detail: The current-best row for run `tailrun-home-admitted-20260328k` inherits the retained frequent-eval blocker posture. Missing or unscored frequent-pack coverage still leaves blocker ids [frequent_exactness_cases_v0, frequent_held_out_exclusions_v0, frequent_throughput_blockers_v0] active, so promotion stays blocked until those eval facts turn green even after cluster closure is otherwise complete.

The current-best row `psion_executor_local_cluster_ledger_row_4080_v1` keeps recovery status `green`. Anything outside `green` or `not_required_same_node` blocks both phase exit and promotion automatically.

The roundtrip closeout packet keeps the current-best export posture explicit as `green` on row `psion_executor_local_cluster_ledger_row_4080_v1`. Until that turns green, the local-cluster phase exit and promotion gates stay blocked instead of relying on review memory. Current cluster closure status is `green`.

The frozen baseline still keeps `reference_linear` explicit as the measured truth anchor for the admitted executor family. If that anchor disappears, both phase exit and promotion stay blocked automatically.

summary: The admitted executor lane now has one canonical auto-block report for local-cluster promotion and phase exits. Missing eval, recovery, export, and `reference_linear` anchor facts are now machine-readable block rows instead of narrative review notes, and promotion-only eval blockers stay distinct from cluster-closure facts.
